template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Tongue Tie in a Newborn (Ankyloglossia)

options.description: Tip of tongue anchored to floor

visual_assessment: Short, tight, or thick lingual frenulum
Tongue unable to protrude beyond lower gum/lip

red_flags: Posterior, thick, or submucosal tongue tie
Feeding difficulties not improving after latch correction
Need for surgical release under specialist care

mild_asymptomatic: Observe and monitor feeding and weight gain
symptomatic: Refer if posterior or complex tie suspected

procedure_details.name: Frenotomy
procedure_details.description: Simple office-based release of tight frenulum
procedure_details.anesthesia: Usually no general anesthesia needed for simple anterior tongue tie
expected_outcome: Improvement in latch and maternal pain

non_pharmacological_advice: Correct breastfeeding position and latch before attributing symptoms solely to tongue tie
Encourage immediate breastfeeding after procedure if performed
Gentle tongue stretching exercises only if specifically advised
Monitor baby’s feeding efficiency and weight gain
Monitor mother for reduction in nipple pain

parent_counseling: Tongue tie is a common cause of breastfeeding difficulty in newborns — जीभ का बंधन (टंग टाई) स्तनपान में कठिनाई का आम कारण है
Mild tongue tie without feeding issues may only need observation — हल्के टंग टाई में सिर्फ़ निगरानी काफ़ी हो सकती है
Frenotomy is generally safe, quick, and effective when indicated — फ्रेनोटॉमी सुरक्षित, जल्दी और असरदार प्रक्रिया है
Most babies feed better soon after release procedure — अधिकतर बच्चे प्रक्रिया के बाद बेहतर दूध पीते हैं
Simple anterior tongue tie usually does not require general anesthesia — साधारण टंग टाई में बेहोशी की ज़रूरत नहीं होती
Seek review if feeding remains poor or bleeding occurs after procedure — अगर दूध पीने में सुधार न हो या खून आए तो दोबारा दिखाएं

follow_up.interval: 3–7 days or earlier if feeding difficulty persists
escalation: Posterior or complex tongue tie suspected

opd_checklist: Difficulty with latch, prolonged feeds, or nipple pain?
Tongue unable to extend past lower gum/lip?
Tight or thick lingual frenulum visible?
warning_signs: Persistent poor latch despite correct positioning — सही स्थिति में भी दूध सही से न पकड़ पाना
Inadequate weight gain — वज़न पर्याप्त न बढ़ना
Maternal nipple trauma or bleeding — माँ के निप्पल में दर्द या खून
Heart-shaped or notched tongue tip — जीभ का दिल जैसा आकार
Clicking sounds during feeds — दूध पीते समय क्लिक की आवाज़
expected_course: Mild anterior tongue tie without feeding difficulty may not require intervention. If frenotomy is performed, immediate improvement in latch is often seen. Post-procedure, babies can breastfeed right away. Posterior tongue tie (Type 3-4) may require ENT or specialist referral. Speech issues may become apparent later if untreated.
key_clinical_points: Always assess feeding function, not just appearance of frenulum
Coryllos classification helps guide management decisions
Type 1-2 (anterior) often amenable to office frenotomy
Maternal lactation support is essential alongside surgical intervention
Re-attachment of frenulum can occur; tongue exercises may help prevent this

severity_assessment.mild: Short frenulum visible but baby feeding well, gaining weight, no maternal nipple pain — observe and reassure
severity_assessment.moderate: Feeding difficulty with poor latch, slow feeds, maternal pain — consider frenotomy, optimize positioning
severity_assessment.severe: Failure to thrive, significant maternal nipple trauma, posterior thick tie — urgent frenotomy or specialist referral
